pattern: A grid is Toeplitz exactly when every cell equals the one diagonally up-left, so a single neighbor comparison per interior cell decides it.
hints: Two cells lie on the same top-left-to-bottom-right diagonal when their row and column indices differ by the same amount.
Rather than walking whole diagonals, notice each cell only needs to match its immediate upper-left neighbor.
For every cell with both a row above and a column to the left, check matrix[i][j] equals matrix[i-1][j-1]; if any pair differs return false, otherwise true.

edge_inputs.description: single row has no diagonal to violate
single column has no diagonal to violate
tiny matrix whose only diagonal disagrees
all zeros at the minimum value bound is Toeplitz
first diagonals match but a deeper one breaks

stress.description: random matrices, most built to be Toeplitz with occasional planted violations
stress.note: roughly half the cases stay Toeplitz, half get one cell perturbed
